place: Altmar CDP, New York, New York

facts.text: Altmar was first settled in 1813 by Scottish immigrant Peter Henderson, who built a log cabin on a 200‑acre riverbank.
The village’s original name, Sand Bank, came from the local term “Alkineaur,” meaning a sand bank along the Salmon River.
Altmar’s Salmon River Fish Hatchery raises more than three million young fish each year for Lake Ontario and Lake Erie stocking programs.
Artist Milton Avery was born in 1885 in what was then called Sand Bank, the area that later became Altmar.
Lake‑effect snow dominates Altmar’s winters, with January averaging about 45 inches of snowfall and peaks sometimes exceeding three inches per hour.
The village dissolved in 2013, leaving Altmar as an unincorporated community within the Town of Albion.
